publishedAbstract: When upgrading an ODF cluster with at-rest encryption enabled from 4.18.z to 4.19.z, the Rook Ceph Operator fails to check if the OSDs are already encrypted. It assumes the OSDs need encryption and forces an unnecessary redeployment of all pre-existing encrypted OSDs. This forces massive, unnecessary data movement (backfilling and recovery) across the entire storage cluster.
publishedTitle: Unnecessary OSD migrations during ODF 4.18 to 4.19 upgrades
